info.title: Email Gateway
info.description: Email gateway that routes emails to Postmark (transactional) or Instantly (broadcast) based on type.

tags.name: Email Routing
tags.description: Route emails to transactional or broadcast providers

success.description: Whether the email was sent successfully

messageId.description: Provider message ID (Postmark messageId or Instantly leadId)

deduplicated.description: True if this response was returned from the idempotency cache (email was not re-sent)

bodyHtml.description: HTML email body for this step

bodyText.description: Plain text email body for this step

daysSinceLastStep.description: Days to wait since the previous step (0 = immediate, step 1 is always 0)

campaignId.description: Campaign ID for tracking and stats grouping

leadId.description: Lead ID for end-to-end tracking. When provided: logged on receipt, forwarded to the downstream provider (Postmark/Instantly), and returned as `messageId` in the response for broadcast sends. Omitting it means email stats cannot be correlated back to the lead.

workflowSlug.description: Workflow slug for tracking and grouping

idempotencyKey.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate sends. If a send with the same key already succeeded, the cached response is returned with `deduplicated: true` and no email is re-sent. Use a value unique per send attempt — e.g. the run ID, or a composite like `{runId}:{nodeId}` when a single workflow run triggers multiple sends.

from.description: Sender address, e.g. "Display Name <email@domain.com>". If omitted, the downstream provider resolves its own default.

campaignId.description: Campaign ID for tracking and stats grouping

leadId.description: Lead ID for end-to-end tracking. When provided: logged on receipt, forwarded to the downstream provider (Postmark/Instantly), and returned as `messageId` in the response for broadcast sends. Omitting it means email stats cannot be correlated back to the lead.

workflowSlug.description: Workflow slug for tracking and grouping

idempotencyKey.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate sends. If a send with the same key already succeeded, the cached response is returned with `deduplicated: true` and no email is re-sent. Use a value unique per send attempt — e.g. the run ID, or a composite like `{runId}:{nodeId}` when a single workflow run triggers multiple sends.

subject.description: Shared email subject line (same thread, follow-ups are Re:)

sequence.description: Email sequence steps sent via Instantly

emailsContacted.description: Total leads contacted (added to campaign / send attempted)

key.description: The value of the groupBy dimension for this bucket. When groupBy=brandId this is the brand UUID; when groupBy=campaignId it is the campaign UUID; when groupBy=workflowSlug it is the workflow slug string; etc.

allOf.description: Transactional (Postmark) stats for this group. Omitted when type=broadcast or when no transactional data exists for this key.
Broadcast (Instantly) stats for this group. Omitted when type=transactional or when no broadcast data exists for this key.

groups.description: One entry per unique value of the groupBy dimension. Each entry contains the key and the stats for that bucket.

GroupedStatsResponse.description: Returned instead of StatsResponse when the groupBy query parameter is set. Groups stats by the requested dimension (brandId, campaignId, workflowSlug, featureSlug, or recipientEmail).

ReplyClassification.description: Classification of the most recent reply: positive, negative, neutral, or null if no reply

contacted.description: Whether this email has been contacted in this scope

sent.description: Whether an email was sent in this scope

delivered.description: Whether an email was delivered in this scope

opened.description: Whether the recipient opened any email in this scope

clicked.description: Whether the recipient clicked any link in this scope

replied.description: Whether the recipient replied in this scope

bounced.description: Whether an email bounced in this scope

unsubscribed.description: Whether the recipient unsubscribed in this scope

lastDeliveredAt.description: ISO timestamp of last delivery in this scope

bounced.description: Whether this email has bounced anywhere

unsubscribed.description: Whether this email has unsubscribed anywhere

GlobalStatus.description: Global signals across all brands and campaigns

byCampaign.description: Per-campaign breakdown (present in brand mode, null in campaign mode)

allOf.description: Status scoped to the given campaign (present in campaign mode, null in brand mode)
Aggregated status across all campaigns for this brand (present in brand mode, null in campaign mode)

ProviderStatus.description: Status from broadcast provider (Instantly). Omitted if no broadcast data exists for this email.

allOf.description: Status from transactional provider (Postmark). Omitted if no transactional data exists for this email.

brandId.description: Brand ID — if present without campaignId, activates brand mode: returns per-campaign breakdown (byCampaign) + aggregated brand status. If both brandId and campaignId are provided, campaign mode takes precedence and brandId is ignored.

campaignId.description: Campaign ID — if present, activates campaign mode: returns status scoped to this specific campaign. Takes precedence over brandId.

items.description: List of emails to check status for

post.description: Send a transactional or broadcast email via the appropriate provider.

value.subject: Your PR coverage report is ready

sequence.bodyHtml: Hi Bob, I have a story for you.
Just following up on my previous email.

broadcastSuccess.summary: Broadcast send — success (messageId is the Instantly leadId)

deduplicated.summary: Idempotency hit — cached response returned, no email re-sent

get.description:
Returns email stats aggregated across providers.

**Without `groupBy`:** returns a flat `StatsResponse` with optional `transactional` and `broadcast` objects.

**With `groupBy`:** returns a `GroupedStatsResponse` — an object with a `groups` array. Each element has a `key` (the value of the groupBy dimension, e.g. a brand UUID when `groupBy=brandId`) and optional `transactional` / `broadcast` stats objects.

Use the `type` parameter to restrict to a single provider (transactional or broadcast).

200.description: Aggregated stats. Shape depends on whether `groupBy` is set — see `StatsResponse` (flat) vs `GroupedStatsResponse` (grouped).

get.summary: Get aggregated email stats (public, no identity headers required)
get.description:
Same behavior as `GET /orgs/stats` but does not require `x-org-id` or any identity headers. Intended for internal services (e.g. leaderboard) that don't have user context.

**Without `groupBy`:** returns a flat `StatsResponse`.

**With `groupBy`:** returns a `GroupedStatsResponse` — `{ groups: [{ key, transactional?, broadcast? }] }`. The `key` is the value of the groupBy dimension (e.g. a brand UUID when `groupBy=brandId`).

post.description:
Batch lookup of delivery status for a list of emails. Requires at least one of `brandId` or `campaignId` in the body.

**Two modes:**

| `brandId` | `campaignId` | Mode | Active fields |
|-----------|-------------|------|---------------|
| present | absent | Brand | `byCampaign` + `brand` + `global` |
| absent | present | Campaign | `campaign` + `global` |
| present | present | Campaign | `campaign` + `global` (brandId ignored) |
| absent | absent | — | 400 error |

**Brand mode** (`brandId` without `campaignId`): returns a `byCampaign` object mapping each campaignId to its `StatusScope`, plus an aggregated `brand` scope (BOOL_OR across campaigns, `replyClassification` from most recent, `lastDeliveredAt` = MAX).

**Campaign mode** (`campaignId` present): returns a single `campaign` scope for that campaign.

Non-applicable fields are always present but set to `null`.

Returns status from both broadcast (Instantly) and transactional (Postmark) providers. If one provider fails, the other's results are still returned. If both fail, returns 502.

**Headers** (`x-brand-id`, `x-campaign-id`, etc.) are tracing/logging only — they are forwarded to downstream services but do NOT influence filtering logic. Filtering is driven exclusively by body fields.

campaignMode.summary: Campaign mode — status for a specific campaign

brandMode.summary: Brand mode — per-campaign breakdown for a brand

200.description: Status results per email, split by provider (broadcast / transactional).

brandMode.summary: Brand mode response — byCampaign breakdown + aggregated brand

400.description: Invalid request — missing brandId and campaignId, empty items, or invalid email

502.description: Upstream service error — both providers failed

post.description: Receives Postmark webhook events and forwards them to the upstream postmark service
Receives Instantly webhook events and forwards them to the upstream instantly service
